2007-07-07.title: Hamilton, Hamilton, Hamilton
2007-07-07.abstract: The Lewis Hamilton effect is felt everywhere. When I left the paddock last night after 10:30 PM – when I took the photo below to provide another view of the new McLaren motor home, as promised – it took 25 minutes to drive back to my hotel in Northampton. When I returned to the track this morning shortly after 8:00 AM, it took an hour and a half. The Hamilton phenomenon is living up to expectation.

2017-07-01.title: State Dept. Restores Job Offers to Students After Diplomat Outcry

2017-07-01.abstract: The president pressed South Korea on trade and made moves that angered China, posing a test of whether he can keep campaign promises while also cooperating on security.

2017-07-25.abstract: Newly released footage of a gunfight that left three Americans dead in November shows a deliberate attack that was initially explained as a mistake.

2017-07-25.title: International Monetary Fund Based in Beijing? Maybe, Its Director Says
2017-07-25.abstract: “We might not be sitting in Washington D.C.,” Christine Lagarde said when asked what the I.M.F. might look like in a decade.

2017-07-25.title: For China’s Global Ambitions, ‘Iran Is at the Center of Everything’
2017-07-25.abstract: Iran’s strategic location makes it a focus, and Chinese investments and entrepreneurs are pouring into the country.

2017-07-26.title: Intelligence Agencies Say North Korean Missile Could Reach U.S. in a Year
2017-07-26.abstract: The estimate significantly shortens the time that analysts believed it would take the regime to develop a weapon capable of reaching the continental United States.

2017-07-26.title: A First: Cardinal Pell Appears in Australian Court on Sexual Charges
2017-07-26.abstract: A lawyer for Cardinal George Pell, one of Pope Francis’ top advisers, said the prelate would plead not guilty to all charges. The hearing lasted six minutes.

2017-07-26.title: Want a Job, Mate? Amazon Is Hiring Aussie Speakers
2017-07-26.abstract: The American tech behemoth may be seeking to tailor its widely used voice recognition system to an Australian audience.

2017-07-26.title: Deadly Building Collapse in India Caused by Illegal Alterations, Police Say
2017-07-26.abstract: The Mumbai police arrested a man affiliated with a local political party and charged him with culpable homicide in the collapse, which killed at least 17 people.

2017-07-26.title: Dr. G. Yunupingu, Australian Aboriginal Singer, Dies at 46
2017-07-26.abstract: The blind, largely self-taught musician produced the best-selling Indigenous album in Australian history and played in concert halls around the world.

2017-07-27.title: Venezuela’s Maduro Remixes ‘Despacito.’ Its Writers Are Not Happy
2017-07-27.abstract: President Nicolás Maduro drew condemnation from the song’s creators after using a version of their tune at a political rally.

2017-07-27.title: Australia’s Greatest (Dying) Global Asset
2017-07-27.abstract: We’re off the coast of Queensland for this week’s newsletter. And it’s not looking good.

2017-07-27.title: U.N. Report Says Saudi-Led Coalition Attacked Migrant Boat Off Yemen
2017-07-27.abstract: The attack in March killed more than 40 people when a helicopter fired on a boat of migrants fleeing the fighting in Yemen.

2017-07-27.title: China and India File Rival Claims Over Tibetan Medicine
2017-07-27.abstract: In Unesco applications, the neighbors are trying to formally tie Tibetan medicine, an ancient practice with a growing commercial value, to their national patrimonies.

2017-07-27.title: Take a Cruise Through the South China Sea on the Forever Happy Princess
2017-07-27.abstract: In the middle of the South China Sea, a cruise ship is helping China project its military and economic might.

2017-07-28.title: Trump Seeks Way to Declare Iran in Violation of Nuclear Deal
2017-07-28.abstract: The president is frustrated that his national security aides have not given him any options on how the United States can leave the Iran nuclear accord.

2017-07-28.title: U.S. Orders Relatives of Embassy Staff in Venezuela to Leave Country
2017-07-28.abstract: The State Department also gave embassy employees the option to leave amid “social unrest” and an upcoming vote to restructure Venezuela’s government.

2017-07-28.title: Aftermath of a Deadly Mumbai Building Collapse
2017-07-28.abstract: More than a dozen people were killed when a five-story apartment building collapsed in Mumbai. A search and rescue operation followed.

2017-07-28.title: Man Sets Himself on Fire in Sydney, Protesting Immigration Policy
2017-07-28.abstract: The protester outside the Department of Immigration in central Sydney was quickly extinguished by the police, officials said, and not seriously hurt.

2017-07-28.title: Who’s in Congo’s Mass Graves? And Why Are Soldiers Guarding Them?
2017-07-28.abstract: Soldiers are stationed at grave sites that could expose a massacre by the security forces, as Congolese civilians are caught between warring sides.

2017-07-29.title: Sharif’s Resignation Comes as U.S. Debates How to Pressure Pakistan on Terrorism
2017-07-29.abstract: The Trump administration’s long-awaited strategy in Afghanistan is stalled in part amid concerns about how to deal with terrorism in Pakistan, whose prime minister resigned Friday.

2017-07-29.title: Pakistan, Ousting Leader, Dashes Hopes for Fuller Democracy
2017-07-29.abstract: Even as some celebrate the ouster of Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif over corruption charges, the event reveals weak spots in Pakistan’s democracy.

2017-07-29.title: Poland’s Court Crisis Cools Off, but It’s Far From Over
2017-07-29.abstract: While the president and the governing party’s leader decide whether they can reach a compromise, continuing protests have energized Poles who fear encroaching authoritarianism.

2017-07-29.title: As Venezuela Prepares to Vote, Some Fear an End to Democracy
2017-07-29.abstract: President Nicolás Maduro’s plan to create a new assembly is expected to give leftists virtually unlimited authority.

2017-07-29.title: Loss of Fertile Land Fuels ‘Looming Crisis’ Across Africa
2017-07-29.abstract: Climate change, soil degradation and rising wealth are shrinking the amount of usable land in Africa. But the number of people who need it is rising fast.

2017-07-30.title: Australia Tightens Airport Security, Saying Police Foiled Bomb Plot
2017-07-30.abstract: The authorities said Sunday that they had disrupted a plan to set off a bomb on a plane, but they offered few details. Four men were arrested.

2017-07-30.title: Sydney’s Original Infinity Pools
2017-07-30.abstract: Take a 360-degree dip in some of the more than 100 ocean baths and rock pools in New South Wales, Australia.

2017-07-30.title: Imran Khan, Strongest Contender to Lead Pakistan, Is No Shoo-In
2017-07-30.abstract: Mr. Khan, a onetime cricket star and anti-American populist, needs to run a strong campaign to succeed Nawaz Sharif as prime minister next year, analysts say.

2017-07-30.title: Global News Quiz: Russia Sanctions, North Korea Milestone, Pakistan Change, Iran Partner
2017-07-30.abstract: Did you stay on top of the most important stories last week? Test your knowledge of international events with our quick quiz, recapping major news headlines and featured articles from across the globe. To take the quiz, click on an answer, and the correct response will be revealed after you choose.

2017-07-30.title: Philippine Mayor Accused of Drug Ties by Duterte Dies in Police Raid
2017-07-30.abstract: Reynaldo Parojinog, mayor of Ozamiz in the southern Philippines, was killed in a firefight at his home, along with his wife and 10 other people, the police said.

2017-07-31.title: Putin’s Bet on a Trump Presidency Backfires Spectacularly
2017-07-31.abstract: If the sanctions passed by Congress last week sent any message to Moscow, it was that Mr. Trump’s hands are tied in dealing with Moscow, probably for years to come.

2017-07-31.title: London’s New Subway Symbolized the Future. Then Came Brexit.
2017-07-31.abstract: Crossrail is a megaproject meant to bind London together. But in the wake of Britain’s vote to leave the European Union, it may signal the end of an ambitious era.

2017-07-31.title: Older Lawn Bowlers Put Their Hands Up to Save Club, With a Beyoncé Twist
2017-07-31.abstract: A beloved lawn bowling club may be at risk of losing its Melbourne home, but thanks to a parody video racking up hits, it has gained some beloved dancers.

2017-07-31.title: New Dangers Stalk Syrian Children Still Haunted by Horrors Under ISIS
2017-07-31.abstract: Children born and raised under Islamic State rule have experienced and witnessed astonishing brutality, even by the cruel standards of the Syrian war.

2017-07-31.title: 2 Die in Attack on Iraqi Embassy in Kabul by Islamic State
2017-07-31.abstract: The attack killed two Afghans, a man and a woman, in at least the third coordinated assault to take place in the Afghan capital over the last few weeks.
